The great salespeople stretch way beyond their own comfort zones. Don't think that these people are courageous. They're not. They're scared to death all the time. They're doing things that make them uncomfortable. They don't tell you they're uncomfortable. They don't tell you they're scared to death. But this is what they do all the time. Why? Because they're driven by this purpose and desire to create an economy for themselves, their businessmen, and their families that they can depend on. So they're willing to get uncomfortable to have some sense of luxury. They don't seek comfort. See, average people seek comfort. I want to be comfortable. I want to be happy. I want to spend time at home. Dude, you can't even take a good luxury vacation. See, the greats want luxury. They seek exceptional lives. Doesn't mean they're going to go spend the money or buy big watches or yachts. Doesn't mean that. It might mean they do that. It might mean they take a big, you know, incredible vacation. But they're seeking a different lifestyle than average. They're not seeking comfortable. And they're willing to make that uncomfortable call and willing to be uncomfortable. Willing to make the sales call where the guy rejects you, won't see you, hates your guts, told you not to call him. Look, they're not interested. The greats aren't interested in average or comfortable. They're interested in greatness and extraordinary. Okay? You up for that one?
